hero.description: Following the historic epic of Qatar in 2022, Morocco's national team prepares to roar once more across American, Canadian, and Mexican soil. Join the journey of a nation guided by passion and the dream of a star.

1970.anecdote: Morocco became the first African nation to qualify through direct qualifiers and made history by matching West Germany (narrow 2-1 defeat) and securing a 1-1 draw against Bulgaria.

1986.anecdote: A resounding success! The Atlas Lions finished first in a tough group ahead of England, Poland, and Portugal (memorable 3-1 win). They were narrowly defeated in the round of 16 by West Germany via a late free kick by Lothar Matthäus (1-0). Morocco became the first African and Arab nation to reach this stage.

1994.anecdote: Despite playing attractive football and having talented players, the team suffered three narrow defeats in a tough group containing Belgium, Saudi Arabia, and the Netherlands.

1998.anecdote: A cruel journey. Led by an outstanding Mustapha Hadji, the Lions charmed the world. After a draw against Norway (2-2) and a loss to Brazil (3-0), they crushed Scotland (3-0). However, they were eliminated at the last minute due to a controversial penalty awarded to Norway against Brazil.

2018.anecdote: Returning after a 20-year absence under Hervé Renard. The Atlas Lions displayed beautiful football but lacked efficiency. They held Spain to a 2-2 draw and suffered a tight 1-0 defeat to Ronaldo's Portugal despite dominating the match.

2022.anecdote: The golden run that shook world football. First in their group (draw against Croatia, wins against Belgium 2-0 and Canada 2-1), Morocco then eliminated Spain on penalties in the round of 16 thanks to Bounou's heroic saves, and Cristiano Ronaldo's Portugal (1-0, En-Nesyri's header) in the quarters. Morocco became the first African and Arab country in history to reach the semi-finals, bowing out with honor against France.

history.title: The World Cup Journey
history.description: Morocco's path in the World Cup is shaped by pioneering achievements and unshakable passion. Relive the founding moments that led to the heights of 2022.

squad.title: The Atlas Lions 2026
squad.description: Explore the official selection for the World Cup. Click on a player to flip their card and view detailed statistics. Missing photos will display a custom avatar in national colors.
squad.photo_instruction: Drop the image file named 'src/assets/[path]' to replace the avatar.

bounou.name: Yassine Bounou
bounou.bio: Penalty shootout hero against Spain in 2022, 'Bono' is one of the most serene and respected goalkeepers in the world, nominated for the Yashin Trophy.

elkajoui.name: Munir El Kajoui
elkajoui.bio: A veteran goalkeeper of absolute reliability, always ready to defend the Moroccan goal with brilliance, now playing for Renaissance de Berkane.

tagnaouti.name: Ahmed Reda Tagnaouti
tagnaouti.bio: Experienced local league goalkeeper playing for ASFAR, a regular at national call-ups and winner of the African Champions League.

hakimi.name: Achraf Hakimi
hakimi.bio: Iconic captain and one of the best right-backs in the world. Known for his supersonic speed and his legendary Panenka penalty against Spain.

mazraoui.name: Noussair Mazraoui
mazraoui.bio: Tactically intelligent defender, playing for Manchester United, capable of adapting to both right and left fullback positions.

aguerd.name: Nayef Aguerd
aguerd.bio: The defensive pillar, now playing for Olympique de Marseille. Elegant in build-up play and dominant in aerial duels.

riad.name: Chadi Riad
riad.bio: Promising young center-back playing in the Premier League for Crystal Palace. His composure and positioning make him the future of the defense.

diop.name: Issa Diop
diop.bio: Rigorous Premier League center-back playing for Fulham, bringing physical power and elite English league experience.

salaheddine.name: Anass Salah-Eddine
salaheddine.bio: Promising young left-back playing for PSV Eindhoven, blessed with excellent ball distribution and rapid forward runs.

elouahdi.name: Zakaria El Ouahdi
elouahdi.bio: Dynamic wing-back from KRC Genk in Belgium, highly recognized for his physical endurance and ability to work the right flank.

belammari.name: Youssef Belammari
belammari.bio: Modern full-back playing for Al Ahly, bringing tactical discipline and high reactivity during defensive transitions.

halhal.name: Redouane Halhal
halhal.bio: Promising central defender playing for KV Mechelen in Belgium, known for his positional awareness and clean passing.

amrabat.name: Sofyan Amrabat
amrabat.bio: The midfield engine and warrior, now playing for Real Betis. His tenacity on the pitch inspires the entire squad.

ounahi.name: Azzedine Ounahi
ounahi.bio: Creative midfielder with silky ball control and exceptional vision, now playing for Spanish club Girona FC.

elkhannouss.name: Bilal El Khannouss
elkhannouss.bio: Attacking midfield prodigy playing in Bundesliga for VfB Stuttgart. Gifted with rare creativity to unlock defensive blocks.

saibari.name: Ismael Saibari
saibari.bio: Powerful and versatile attacking midfielder from PSV Eindhoven, combining physical strength with a clinical strike.

bouaddi.name: Ayyoub Bouaddi
bouaddi.bio: The new 18-year-old rising star of LOSC Lille. Blessed with remarkable calm and tactical maturity that won over the national team staff.

elaynaoui.name: Neil El Aynaoui
elaynaoui.bio: Relentless box-to-box midfielder playing for AS Roma in Italy, known for his recovery work and transition play.

talbi.name: Chemsdine Talbi
talbi.bio: Agile young attacking midfielder playing for Sunderland in England, bringing technical flair and creativity.

elmourabet.name: Samir El Mourabet
elmourabet.bio: Technical playmaker playing for RC Strasbourg, recognized for tactical intelligence and excellent passing vision.

gessime.name: Yassine Gessime
gessime.bio: Fast and direct attacking midfielder for RC Strasbourg, capable of stretching defenses along the flanks.

diaz.name: Brahim Díaz
diaz.bio: The dazzling attacking playmaker from Real Madrid. Brings world-class creativity, acceleration, and technique to the Lions' offense.

rahimi.name: Soufiane Rahimi
rahimi.bio: Top scorer of the Paris 2024 Olympic Games with Al-Ain FC. An ultra-combative forward, highly clinical in front of goal.

ezzalzouli.name: Abdessamad Ezzalzouli
ezzalzouli.bio: Dynamic and unpredictable winger playing for Real Betis, Olympic medalist, specialist in 1v1 duels and dribbles.

elkaabi.name: Ayoub El Kaabi
elkaabi.bio: Clinical striker for Olympiacos and hero of their historic Conference League victory. An outstanding finisher with exceptional movement.

amaimouni.name: Ayoub Amaimouni
amaimouni.bio: Promising young forward playing in Bundesliga for Eintracht Frankfurt, combining physical power with clinical finishing.

legends.title: The Temple of Legends
legends.description: A tribute to the eternal figures of Moroccan football. Through their talent and bravery, they paved the way for future generations on the international stage.

hadji.name: Mustapha Hadji
hadji.quote: Wearing this red and green shirt with the star has always been the greatest honor of my life. We played with heart for all our people.
hadji.bio: The symbol of the 1998 generation. His bicycle kick against Norway and his silky playstyle are forever etched in international football history.

naybet.name: Noureddine Naybet
naybet.quote: Rigor and discipline are the foundations of success. Being an Atlas Lion requires total sacrifice on the pitch.
naybet.bio: One of the greatest defenders in African football history. A Deportivo La Coruña legend with whom he won La Liga, he captained Morocco in 2 World Cups.

bassir.name: Salaheddine Bassir
bassir.quote: The roar of the Moroccan crowd is an invisible force that pushes you beyond your physical limits.
bassir.bio: A swift striker, extremely clinical in front of goal. His two legendary goals against Scotland in the 1998 World Cup in France are part of the nation's sports heritage.

zaki.name: Badou Zaki
zaki.quote: A goalkeeper must be the general of his army. If he doesn't tremble, no one trembles.
zaki.bio: The legendary goalkeeper and captain of the 1986 squad. His decisive saves against England and Portugal propelled Morocco to the second round in Mexico.

staff.title: The Ranya Coaching Staff
staff.description: The tactical structure and mental strength of the Lions are built upon a dedicated and passionate team of professionals.
staff.federation_seal_text: In coordination with the National Technical Directorate (DTN), this team oversees physical preparation, tactical video analysis, and nutritional programs for the Atlas Lions.

ouahbi.philosophy: Rigorous development of youth and seamless transition to the elite global level. Moroccan football is harvesting the fruits of top-tier planning.
ouahbi.bio: Appointed head coach on March 5, 2026, to succeed Walid Regragui. A highly successful former coach of the Moroccan U20 side (leading them to a world title in 2025), Mohamed Ouahbi embodies strategic continuity and the integration of a talented young generation for the 2026 World Cup.

stadiums.title: Stadiums & Fervor 2026
stadiums.description: From the legendary Azteca to the modern temple of New York, discover the venues where the chants of Moroccan supporters will echo.

metlife.desc: The host stadium for the grand final of the 2026 World Cup. A monumental arena equipped with cutting-edge technology, where Moroccan fans in North America promise an electric atmosphere. Location of the historic opening match draw against Brazil (1-1).

sofi.desc: A modern architectural masterpiece featuring a suspended double-sided circular screen. This futuristic arena will host some of the tournament's most spectacular matches.

bcplace.desc: A stadium featuring a magnificent cable-supported retractable roof. Located in the heart of the Canadian city, it offers a stunning setting and incredible acoustics for supporter chants.

azteca.desc: The legendary temple of world football, the first stadium to host three World Cups. This is where Morocco wrote its legend in 1970 and 1986. A sacred place steeped in history.

footer.brand_desc: An immersive showcase portal dedicated to the journey of the Moroccan national football team for the FIFA World Cup 2026.
footer.fed_text: To follow the official updates from the FRMF (Royal Moroccan Football Federation), visit the federation portal.
footer.copy_notice: Non-official demo website for portfolio showcase. All rights reserved.

matches.title: The Lions' Path
matches.description: Follow the national team's complete journey, from the qualifiers to the World Cup 2026 group stage.

news.title: Latest Updates
news.description: Stay informed about the Atlas Lions, from physical preparation to behind-the-scenes camp updates.
news.art1_desc: The technical staff under Mohamed Ouahbi has launched the high-intensity physical camp under the Florida sun.
news.art2_desc: The Real Madrid playmaker spoke about the team's cohesion and their overflowing ambition for this World Cup.
news.art3_desc: Moroccan fan associations in North America are preparing giant tifos and historic chants to welcome the Lions.
